items.title: UPC and Partial‑Problems
items.title_zh: UPC 中央分院在钉子专利案中适用部分问题法
items.title_en: UPC Munich Applies Partial-Problem Approach to Reject Nail Patent for Lack of Inventive Step
items.summary: The partial-problems approach to inventive step is a well-known part of European patent practice, not least as it is used routinely by...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 中央分院（慕尼黑）在涉及 EP4019790 号钉子的诉讼中，首次适用“部分问题法”评估创造性。法院认定权利要求中的两个区别特征（钉尖角度与纵向凹槽）分别解决独立技术问题且无协同效应，属于简单叠加。因此，法院单独评估各特征创造性，最终因两者均显而易见而判定整体缺乏创造性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Central Division in Munich has applied the partial-problems approach to inventive step in case UPC_CFI_836/2024 concerning European patent EP4019790. The patent covered a nail featuring a tip angle exceeding 40 degrees and longitudinal shaft grooves. The court determined these features addressed technically independent problems—reducing splitting during driving and increasing pull-out resistance after insertion—without functional interaction or synergy. Consequently, the claim was treated as a mere aggregation of features. Each feature was assessed in isolation against prior art and general technical knowledge. Finding both the specific tip angle and the grooves independently obvious, the court ruled the entire claim lacked inventive step. This decision aligns UPC practice with established EPO standards regarding combinations of non-interacting technical features.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请专利时，应避免将无协同效应的技术特征简单堆砌。面对 UPC 诉讼，需警惕对方利用“部分问题法”拆解组合发明，提前准备特征间协同效应的技术证据以抗辩无效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling confirms that the UPC closely follows EPO jurisprudence regarding the assessment of inventive step for multi-feature claims. Practitioners should ensure that combination patents demonstrate clear synergistic effects or functional interactions between components. In litigation, parties must be prepared to argue against the disaggregation of features by providing robust technical evidence of interdependence. Failure to establish such synergy may lead courts to evaluate features in isolation, significantly increasing the risk of invalidity findings based on isolated obviousness.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 采纳 EPO 惯例，对无协同特征拆分评估创造性", "法院认定钉尖角度与凹槽解决不同阶段问题，无功能交互", "因特征均为简单叠加且各自显而易见，专利被判无效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Munich adopted the EPO's partial-problems approach, treating non-synergistic features as an aggregation rather than a unified invention.", "The court found no functional interaction between the nail tip angle and shaft grooves, as they solved distinct technical problems at different stages.", "Since each feature was deemed independently obvious based on prior art, the combined claim was invalidated for lacking overall inventive step."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免无协同特征简单叠加，以防被拆分认定显而易见", "诉讼中需重点举证技术特征间的功能交互与协同效应"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure combination patents clearly document synergistic effects to prevent features from being evaluated in isolation during validity challenges.", "Practice note: When drafting or litigating, prioritize evidence demonstrating functional interdependence between claimed features to counter aggregation arguments.", "What to monitor: Track further UPC decisions applying the partial-problems approach to identify consistent thresholds for proving technical synergy."]

The court found no functional interaction between the nail tip angle and shaft grooves, as they solved distinct technical problems at different stages.
Since each feature was deemed independently obvious based on prior art, the combined claim was invalidated for lacking overall inventive step.

Practice note: When drafting or litigating, prioritize evidence demonstrating functional interdependence between claimed features to counter aggregation arguments.
What to monitor: Track further UPC decisions applying the partial-problems approach to identify consistent thresholds for proving technical synergy.

items.title: UPC: Supplementing expert opinion after inspection not possible
items.title_zh: UPC 裁定：勘验后不得补充专家意见
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Denies Supplementary Expert Opinion After Evidence Preservation Inspection
items.summary: Van Loon had applied to inspect and preserve evidence at Inverquark’s exhibition stand in advance of a potential main action, which went...
items.ai_summary_zh: 杜塞尔多夫地方法院在 Van Loon 诉 Inverquark 案中裁定，被告无权要求法院委托的专家在证据保全勘验后出具补充意见。法院指出，单方命令后的程序权利可通过复核申请保障，且该专家非规则 187 条定义的“法院专家”，无法律依据支持补充报告。实质准确性异议应在主诉讼中提出。
items.ai_summary_en: The Düsseldorf Local Division of the Unified Patent Court ruled in Van Loon v. Inverquark that a defendant cannot request a supplementary opinion from a court-commissioned expert following an evidence preservation inspection. The court rejected the defendant’s argument that the initial report inadequately reflected their product, finding no legal basis for such a supplement. The panel clarified that the expert was not appointed under Rule 187 of the Rules of Procedure, meaning parties lack the right to comment on the report at this stage. Procedural safeguards are instead provided through the option to request a review under Rule 197.3. The court emphasized that preservation measures aim to secure information quickly, while substantive accuracy disputes must be addressed in subsequent main proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在面对 UPC 证据保全时，应重视保护信质量以预防勘验偏差。若对专家报告有异议，切勿试图在保全阶段要求补充，而应策略性地将技术事实争议保留至主诉讼阶段进行实质性抗辩。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the procedural boundaries of evidence preservation actions before the UPC. Parties should recognize that these measures are fact-finding tools rather than venues for resolving substantive patent disputes. Consequently, efforts to challenge expert findings through supplementary reports at the preservation stage are likely to fail. Instead, litigants should focus on ensuring protective letters are technically precise to prevent misinterpretation during inspections. Strategic resources should be allocated to preparing robust defenses for the main proceedings, where detailed technical arguments and objections to the expert’s conclusions can be fully adjudicated.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院驳回被告要求专家出具补充意见的请求，认定缺乏法律依据", "证据保全旨在快速获取信息，实质争议应留待后续主诉讼解决", "被告对报告准确性的质疑虽被记录，但需在主程序中重新审议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court dismissed the request for a supplementary expert report, determining there is no legal basis for ordering additional opinions after an inspection.", "Evidence preservation procedures prioritize rapid information gathering, deferring substantive technical accuracy challenges to future main litigation.", "Although the defendant's specific questions were noted, they must be re-evaluated and argued during the subsequent main proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["保护信是应对单方勘验的关键，需详尽描述技术特征以防误读", "针对专家报告的实质异议，应制定主诉讼阶段的详细抗辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Understand that evidence preservation is distinct from trial; do not expect final resolution of technical facts at this early stage.", "Practice note: Draft protective letters with high technical specificity to mitigate risks of inaccurate expert descriptions during ex-parte inspections.", "What to monitor: Track how courts handle similar requests for supplementary reports and observe the treatment of preserved evidence in upcoming main actions."]
core_points_en: The court dismissed the request for a supplementary expert report, determining there is no legal basis for ordering additional opinions after an inspection.
Evidence preservation procedures prioritize rapid information gathering, deferring substantive technical accuracy challenges to future main litigation.
Although the defendant's specific questions were noted, they must be re-evaluated and argued during the subsequent main proceedings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Understand that evidence preservation is distinct from trial; do not expect final resolution of technical facts at this early stage.
Practice note: Draft protective letters with high technical specificity to mitigate risks of inaccurate expert descriptions during ex-parte inspections.
What to monitor: Track how courts handle similar requests for supplementary reports and observe the treatment of preserved evidence in upcoming main actions.

items.title: Unified Patent Court Insights: Claim Construction
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院洞察：权利要求构建与功能性限定解释
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Clarifies Product vs. System Claims: Functional 'Mountable' Language Does Not Require External Apparatus
items.summary: A recent decision from the Düsseldorf Local Division in UPC_CFI_351/2024 provides an interesting look at the Unified Patent Court's (UPC)...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫分部在 UPC_CFI_351/2024 号案件中，就打印机供粉容器的侵权纠纷作出裁决。核心争议在于“可安装”一词是否要求外部设备（打印机）实际存在。法院裁定，此类功能性语言仅定义产品适用性而非系统组成部分，确认单独销售容器构成对产品权利要求的直接侵权，厘清了产品claim与系统claim的界限。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf Local Division issued a significant ruling in UPC_CFI_351/2024 regarding claim construction for printer supply containers. The central dispute concerned whether the term 'detachably mountable' required the physical presence of an external apparatus, such as a printer, to establish infringement. The defendants argued this language transformed the claim into a system claim, meaning standalone toner bottles could not infringe. The Court rejected this interpretation, ruling that functional terms like 'mountable' define the product's suitability for intended use rather than mandating the co-existence of external components. Consequently, the claim was construed as a product claim covering the container alone. This decision clarifies the boundary between product and system claims, confirming that direct infringement can occur through the sale of standalone components even when functional language references external devices.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需注意，即便仅销售适配特定设备的配件，若功能描述符合专利限定，仍可能在欧盟面临直接侵权风险。建议在产品设计及说明书中审慎规避他人专利的功能性表述，并提前开展 FTO 分析。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights how functional claim language can broaden patent protection scope within the UPC framework. Rights holders may leverage terms describing compatibility or mounting capabilities to assert direct infringement against suppliers of standalone accessories, without needing to prove the presence of the host device. For manufacturers and exporters, particularly those supplying interoperable hardware components, this underscores the critical importance of thorough Freedom to Operate (FTO) analyses. Companies should carefully review competitor patents for functional limitations that might capture their products as standalone items. Proactive design-around strategies must account for these broader interpretations to mitigate litigation risks in European markets where the UPC holds jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院裁定功能性限定词仅界定产品用途适应性，无需外部设备共存", "明确区分产品权利要求与系统权利要求，单独部件销售亦可侵权", "被告关于必须包含打印机组件才能实现发明的抗辩未被法院采纳"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["功能性描述可能扩大保护范围，单独销售配件亦存直接侵权风险", "对欧出口硬件需严格排查专利权利要求，避免触碰功能性限定红线"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Functional descriptions may expand protection scope, creating direct infringement risks for standalone accessory sales.", "Practice note: Conduct rigorous FTO analysis on hardware exports to Europe, specifically scrutinizing functional claim limitations.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on claim construction, particularly regarding functional language and product-system distinctions."]
core_points_en: Functional terms like 'mountable' define product suitability for intended use, not the mandatory presence of external apparatus.
The Court distinguished product claims from system claims, finding that standalone component sales can constitute direct infringement.
Defendant arguments requiring external device co-existence for invention realization were rejected by the Düsseldorf Local Division.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Functional descriptions may expand protection scope, creating direct infringement risks for standalone accessory sales.
Practice note: Conduct rigorous FTO analysis on hardware exports to Europe, specifically scrutinizing functional claim limitations.
What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on claim construction, particularly regarding functional language and product-system distinctions.

items.title: How to plan successful IP mediation
items.title_zh: 如何规划成功的知识产权调解策略
items.title_en: Strategic IP Mediation in Europe: Leveraging New EUIPO and UPC ADR Frameworks
items.summary: Mediation is an increasingly attractive option to resolve IP disputes, especially for SMEs, individuals and universities. But it is important that organisations are well prepared and proactive, says Cyrille Rousseau of Dennemeyer & Associates.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，正成为解决知识产权纠纷的重要选项。重点介绍欧盟层面新机遇：欧盟知识产权局调解中心已于 2023 年启动并扩展服务；统一专利法院专利调解仲裁中心将于 2026 年启用。文章强调各方需充分准备并以诚意参与，以实现双赢并创造未来商机。
items.ai_summary_en: Mediation is emerging as a highly effective mechanism for resolving intellectual property disputes, offering significant advantages over traditional litigation. It provides cost efficiency, confidentiality, and the flexibility to address cross-jurisdictional conflicts while fostering commercially oriented solutions. This approach is particularly beneficial for SMEs, individuals, and academic institutions seeking win-win outcomes that preserve business relationships. The European landscape is evolving with new institutional support. The EU Intellectual Property Office expanded its Mediation Centre services in June 2025 to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court will inaugurate its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026, establishing a dedicated forum for alternative dispute resolution in patent matters across Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Organizations operating in Europe should strategically integrate these new mediation frameworks into their broader IP enforcement and defense strategies. Utilizing the expanded EUIPO services and the upcoming UPC platform can significantly reduce the financial burden and time associated with cross-border litigation. Parties must prepare thoroughly and engage in good faith to maximize the potential for commercially viable settlements. Proactive planning allows rights holders to mitigate the risks of public disclosure inherent in court proceedings while preserving valuable commercial partnerships. Early adoption of these alternative dispute resolution mechanisms positions companies to navigate complex IP landscapes more efficiently and sustainably.

items.title: EU Pharma Package: The “Bolar” Exemption Compromise Proposal
items.title_zh: 欧盟制药一揽子计划：博拉尔豁免妥协提案分析
items.title_en: EU Pharma Package: Analysis of the Compromise Proposal on the Bolar Exemption
items.summary: In our sixth alert in this EU Pharma Package Series, we provided an analysis of the history and interpretation issues for another highly debated topic: the “Bolar” exemption.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析欧盟制药一揽子计划中备受争议的“博拉尔”豁免条款。该豁免允许仿制药企在专利期内进行上市前准备而不侵权。妥协文本扩大了物质和主体范围，涵盖定价、报销及第三方API供应商活动，但地域范围仍不明确。此外，提案明确禁止专利链接，旨在解决成员国间解释不一的问题，确保法律适用的统一性。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the controversial 'Bolar' exemption within the EU Pharma Package, a critical safe harbor allowing generic and biosimilar manufacturers to conduct pre-launch activities without patent or supplementary protection certificate infringement. The compromise text significantly expands both the material and personal scope of this exemption. It now explicitly covers pricing, reimbursement negotiations, health technology assessments, and tender participation, while also extending protections to third-party active pharmaceutical ingredient suppliers. However, the territorial scope remains ambiguous in the final text. To prevent divergent interpretations among Member States—a persistent issue under current legislation—uniform transposition is essential. Furthermore, the proposal explicitly prohibits patent linkage mechanisms, reinforcing the European Commission’s stance that such practices are unlawful and anticompetitive, thereby aiming to ensure legal certainty and market access efficiency across the Union.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企应密切关注豁免范围扩大带来的合规机遇，利用新规则优化上市前策略。同时，需警惕地域适用差异，加强内部合规审查，确保供应链活动符合目标国最新法规，规避潜在的专利侵权诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should assess how the expanded material scope impacts pre-launch strategies for generics and biosimilars. The inclusion of pricing, reimbursement, and tender participation provides greater clarity for commercial preparations but requires careful navigation of national implementations. Companies must monitor how individual Member States transpose these rules, as divergent interpretations could create compliance risks despite the goal of harmonization. Additionally, the explicit prohibition of patent linkage offers a more predictable regulatory environment for market entry. Supply chain actors, particularly third-party API suppliers, should review their operations against the new personal scope to ensure they fall within the protected safe harbor, mitigating potential patent infringement liabilities during the development phase.

He has been involved in multi-jurisdictional litigation including the UK Supreme Court cases Conor Medsystems vs […] The post CMS strengthens London IP practice with McCarthy Denning partner appeared first on JUVE Patent .

items.ai_summary_en: Tom Carver officially joined CMS Cameron McKenna’s London office as a partner on April 20. He brings extensive experience across patents, trademarks, and design rights, complemented by a degree in genetics from the University of Nottingham. This dual qualification allows him to handle complex technical disputes effectively. Carver has previously participated in multi-jurisdictional litigation, including significant cases before the UK Supreme Court, such as Conor Medsystems. His appointment is strategically aimed at strengthening the firm’s capabilities in managing intricate intellectual property controversies and portfolio administration. The move enhances CMS's capacity to serve clients requiring sophisticated legal support in high-stakes IP matters, particularly within the life sciences sector where technical expertise is critical for successful litigation outcomes.

Evaluating the specific litigation approaches of such newly appointed partners can help optimize overseas rights protection and response layouts, ensuring robust preparation for high-value, technically complex IP conflicts.

items.title: UPC Weekly - UPC Court of Appeal weighs in on blood glucose monitoring preliminary injunctions
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉院裁定血糖监测产品临时禁令案件
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Issues Conflicting Rulings on Glucose Monitoring Preliminary Injunctions
items.summary: Matthew Naylor gives an update on the UPC’s approach to costs, security for costs and the courts’ attitude to litigation insurance.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院针对同一连续血糖监测产品的两项临时禁令申请作出相反裁决，维持一项并撤销另一项。案件涉及对专利权利要求的解释范围，法院驳回被告将权利要求限缩至特定实施例的主张，确认了专利侵权可能性及颁发禁令的必要性，为医疗器械领域诉讼提供新指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal has issued divergent decisions regarding preliminary injunctions for the same continuous glucose monitoring product, upholding one order while overturning another from the same Local Division. These rulings provide critical insights into claim interpretation and procedural requirements at the UPC. The court rejected the defendant’s argument to narrowly construe the patent claims to a specific embodiment, affirming a broader scope based on the distinction between independent and dependent claims. The decisions clarify that granting an inter partes preliminary injunction requires satisfaction of several key criteria: probable infringement, imminent or actual infringing acts, absence of undue delay by the patentee, necessity of the measure, and a favorable balance of interests. This case highlights the strategic importance of precise claim construction and timely enforcement actions in European medical device litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口欧洲需警惕 UPC 临时禁令风险，避免权利要求解释不利。建议提前开展自由实施分析，评估专利稳定性，并在遭遇诉讼时积极主张合理的权利要求解释范围以争取抗辩空间。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the strategic volatility of preliminary injunction proceedings before the Unified Patent Court. Parties involved in medical device disputes must prioritize robust freedom-to-operate analyses and early assessment of patent stability to mitigate the risk of rapid injunctive relief. When facing litigation, constructing a defense around precise claim construction is essential; arguing for a narrower interpretation may be viable if supported by dependent claims but risks failure if the independent claims are clearly broader. Litigants should also ensure strict adherence to timing requirements to avoid findings of undue delay, which can critically impact the court's decision on the necessity and balance of interests for granting interim measures.

items.title_en: Former August Debouzy and Aramis Litigators Launch French IP Boutique Pairs Law
items.summary: Anaïs Pallut and Annabelle Divoy’s new firm Pairs Law will cover the full range of IP matters, including patents, trademarks, copyright, designs and software, but with a clear focus on patent litigation. Both lawyers have several years of experience in well-established patent litigation teams such as August Debouzy, Aramis and Hoyng ROKH Monegier. Experience across […] The post Former litigators of August Debouzy and Aramis join forces for new French IP boutique appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 前 August Debouzy 和 Aramis 律所的诉讼律师 Anaïs Pallut 与 Annabelle Divoy 联合创立法国新精品律所 Pairs Law。该所业务涵盖专利、商标、版权等各类知识产权，但明确将专利诉讼作为核心专注领域。两位创始人均在知名专利诉讼团队拥有多年实战经验，旨在为市场提供高端诉讼服务。
items.ai_summary_en: Anaïs Pallut and Annabelle Divoy have established Pairs Law, a new French intellectual property boutique firm. While the practice covers the full spectrum of IP rights, including trademarks, copyright, designs, and software, it maintains a distinct strategic focus on patent litigation. Both founders bring extensive experience from prominent litigation teams at major firms such as August Debouzy, Aramis, and Hoyng ROKH Monegier. Their objective is to deliver high-end dispute resolution services by leveraging their specialized background in complex patent matters. This development reflects a broader trend of experienced practitioners forming specialized entities to address specific market needs within the European IP landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 法国专利诉讼专业服务细分化，中企在法涉诉时需关注新兴精品所动向。建议出口企业评估其专业优势，将其纳入潜在代理或应对策略考量，以优化在欧洲核心市场的法律资源配置。
items.ai_insight_en: The emergence of specialized boutiques like Pairs Law indicates a deepening segmentation in the French patent litigation market. For companies operating in Europe, particularly those with significant R&D or product portfolios, this shift offers alternative avenues for legal representation beyond traditional large firms. In-house counsel should evaluate whether these niche practitioners provide superior cost-efficiency or specialized technical depth for specific patent disputes. Integrating such emerging players into vendor lists can optimize legal resource allocation and provide flexible options for managing complex cross-border IP enforcement strategies in key European jurisdictions.

items.title: EU copyright law roundup – first trimester of 2026
items.title_zh: 2026 年第一季度欧盟版权法综述：关键判例与政策
items.title_en: EU Copyright Law Roundup Q1 2026: CJEU Rulings on Critical Editions and Geoblocking
items.summary: Welcome to our first roundup for 2026. In the first trimester of this year, several important developments have taken place in the EU copyright law arena. As our regular audience will know, in this series we have been reporting on CJEU judgments, Advocate General (AG) Opinions (if any) and significant policy developments.You can read the previous roundups here.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文综述 2026 年首季欧盟版权法动态，重点分析两起 CJEU 相关案件。一是 Anne Frank Fonds 案中总法官就向公众传播权、地理封锁及 VPN 规避发表意见；二是 Institutul G. Călinescu 案判决，确立公共领域作品批判性版本若体现作者个性智力创造，可获版权保护，重申 Cofemel 案双重标准。
items.ai_summary_en: This overview covers significant EU copyright developments from the first quarter of 2026, focusing on key Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) activities. It examines Advocate General Rantos’s opinion in the Anne Frank Fonds case, which addresses the right of communication to the public, geoblocking measures, and potential circumvention via VPNs. Additionally, it analyzes the CJEU judgment in Institutul G. Călinescu, concerning whether critical editions of public domain works qualify for copyright protection. The summary highlights how these decisions impact the interpretation of originality standards and the scope of exclusive rights within the digital single market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业整理公版素材或开发数字内容时，需注意批判性编辑可能产生新版权。涉及跨境流媒体服务应关注地理封锁合规风险，避免因技术规避手段引发侵权纠纷，建议提前评估内容独创性。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders managing public domain content or developing digital archives should carefully assess whether editorial interventions, such as annotations or critical apparatuses, meet the threshold for originality under EU law. If such additions constitute independent intellectual creations, they may attract separate copyright protection, affecting downstream licensing strategies. Furthermore, operators of cross-border streaming services must remain vigilant regarding geoblocking compliance. The ongoing scrutiny of VPN-based circumvention suggests that technical measures intended to bypass territorial restrictions could expose providers to infringement claims, necessitating robust legal risk assessments.

The CJEU ruled that critical editions of public domain works may enjoy copyright protection if they reflect the author's personality through free and creative choices.
The Court reaffirmed the Cofemel standard, requiring subject matter to be an intellectual creation expressed with sufficient precision and objectivity.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess whether editorial enhancements to public domain materials create new, protectable copyright interests requiring distinct licensing.
Practice note: Evaluate the originality of critical editions against the Cofemel criteria to determine eligibility for copyright protection.
What to monitor: Track evolving jurisprudence on geoblocking enforcement and the legality of VPN circumvention techniques.

items.title: ‘Drip pricing’ lands AA driving schools with £4.2 million fine as CMA makes use of its consumer enforcement powers
items.title_zh: 滴灌定价致 AA 驾校被罚 420 万英镑
items.title_en: CMA Imposes £4.2m Fine on AA Driving Schools for Drip Pricing Breach
items.summary: Earlier today, the CMA announced that it has fined the AA and BSM driving schools (both of which are owned by the AA) the sum of £4.2 million for drip p...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国竞争与市场管理局（CMA）首次行使直接执法权，对 AA 及其旗下 BSM 驾校的“滴灌定价”行为处以 420 万英镑罚款，并责令退还消费者 76 万英镑。该案标志着 CMA 依据《数字市场、竞争和消费者法》实质性打击隐瞒强制费用的行为，预示未来将加强对恶劣商业惯例的执法力度。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has imposed a £4.2 million fine on AA and BSM driving schools for employing drip pricing practices. This action marks the first use of the CMA’s direct enforcement powers under the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act (DMCCA) to penalize substantive breaches of consumer protection law. The regulator ordered refunds exceeding £760,000 to affected learner drivers. The violation involved omitting a mandatory £3 booking fee from the initial headline price, revealing it only later in the transaction process. This case signals a significant shift in regulatory strategy, demonstrating the CMA’s readiness to utilize new statutory tools to secure consumer redress and impose financial penalties. With twelve additional live cases underway, businesses operating in the UK should anticipate intensified scrutiny of online pricing transparency and hidden charges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This enforcement action highlights the immediate operational risks associated with non-compliant pricing displays under the new DMCCA framework. Companies selling to UK consumers must ensure that all mandatory charges are included in the headline price presented at the start of the purchasing journey. Regulatory tolerance for hidden fees is diminishing, with the CMA actively pursuing egregious practices. Businesses should conduct immediate audits of their online checkout flows and advertising materials to identify any undisclosed mandatory costs. Failure to align with these transparency requirements may result in substantial financial penalties and reputational damage. Proactive compliance reviews are essential to mitigate exposure as the CMA expands its portfolio of consumer enforcement cases.

items.title: EUIPO SME Fund 2026: Reduce Trademark Costs For SMEs
items.title_zh: EUIPO 2026 中小企业基金：降低商标申请成本
items.title_en: EUIPO SME Fund 2026: Reducing Trademark Registration Costs for Eligible Enterprises
items.summary: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (“EUIPO”) SME Fund 2026 enables eligible small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to reclaim part of the cost of trademark registration...
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO 将于 2026 年 2 月启动中小企业基金，符合条件的企业可报销部分国家及欧盟商标注册费。该举措旨在应对日益激烈的品牌竞争，鼓励中小企业尽早布局知识产权。文章对比了马耳他国内商标与欧盟商标的覆盖范围，强调早期申请对规避冲突及获取全域保护的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) will launch the SME Fund in February 2026, enabling eligible small and medium-sized enterprises to reclaim a portion of their trademark registration fees. This initiative covers costs for both national applications within Member States and European Union Trademarks (EUTM). The program aims to mitigate rising brand competition by encouraging early intellectual property protection strategies. By offering financial reimbursement, the EUIPO supports businesses in securing their brand identity without significant upfront expenditure. The analysis highlights the strategic advantage of EUTMs, which provide immediate coverage across all twenty-seven EU Member States, contrasting this with single-country filings. Early application is emphasized as a critical measure to avoid potential conflicts, reduce opposition risks, and ensure comprehensive legal protection for brand assets throughout the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国赴欧中小企业应密切关注该基金申请窗口，利用财政补贴降低欧盟商标布局成本。建议结合市场规划，优先选择欧盟商标以实现广域保护，避免因费用顾虑延迟注册而遭遇抢注或侵权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should evaluate the EUIPO SME Fund as a viable mechanism to lower the financial barriers associated with European brand protection. Leveraging these subsidies allows for more robust IP portfolio management without straining operational budgets. Companies must strategically weigh the cost-efficiency of unitary EU trademarks against individual national registrations to align with their expansion goals. Delaying registration due to cost concerns increases exposure to bad-faith filings and enforcement challenges. Proactive planning ensures that brand assets are secured before market entry, providing a stronger legal foundation for commercial activities and dispute resolution within the EU jurisdiction.

items.title: Distribution issues: Italian Competition Authority fines Morellato for resale price maintenance and online marketplace restrictions
items.title_zh: 意竞争局因转售限价及平台禁令罚没莫雷拉托
items.title_en: Italian Authority Fines Morellato for Resale Price Maintenance and Discriminatory Online Marketplace Bans
items.summary: On 31 March 2026, the Italian Competition Authority (ICA) announced that it had imposed a fine of just under €26 million on Morellato, a leading Italian...
items.ai_summary_zh: 意大利竞争管理局对钟表商莫雷拉托处以近二千六百万欧元罚款，认定其实施转售价格维持及歧视性禁止经销商使用电商平台。尽管欧盟法院允许奢侈品基于品牌形象限制平台销售，但本案因品牌方自身利用该平台而构成歧视，违反比例原则。
items.ai_summary_en: On 31 March 2026, the Italian Competition Authority (ICA) imposed a fine of nearly €26 million on Morellato, a prominent manufacturer of watches and jewellery. The authority found that Morellato engaged in resale price maintenance by preventing distributors from freely setting resale prices. Additionally, Morellato prohibited authorised distributors from selling its products on online marketplaces such as Amazon and eBay since 2018. While the company cited the CJEU’s Coty judgment to justify these restrictions for brand image preservation, the ICA determined the ban was applied discriminatorily because Morellato itself sold via these platforms. This case underscores that vertical restraints, particularly fixed pricing and selective distribution limits, remain high-priority enforcement targets under EU and national competition laws.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企应审查分销协议，避免设定最低转售价。若限制经销商入驻电商平台，须确保自身未在同一平台销售，以防被认定为歧视性限制而招致反垄断重罚。
items.ai_insight_en: Suppliers operating selective distribution networks in Europe must rigorously audit their commercial agreements to ensure compliance with competition law. Agreements containing clauses that fix or set minimum resale prices pose significant legal risk and should be removed immediately. Regarding online sales, brands may restrict marketplace use only if such measures are proportionate and applied consistently. If a supplier actively sells through third-party online marketplaces, it generally cannot prohibit its authorised distributors from doing so without facing allegations of discriminatory treatment. Companies should align internal sales strategies with distributor policies to avoid violating vertical restraint guidelines and incurring substantial fines.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["固定或最低转售价格属核心限制，系近年欧盟及各成员国执法重点", "禁止使用电商平台需统一非歧视，品牌方自用则不得禁止经销商使用", "该案警示选择性分销体系中网络销售限制的合规边界与比例原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Fixed or minimum resale prices constitute hardcore restrictions and remain a primary enforcement priority for European competition authorities.", "Online marketplace bans must be applied uniformly and non-discriminatorily; suppliers cannot restrict distributors while using the same platforms themselves.", "This decision highlights critical compliance boundaries regarding proportionality and non-discrimination within selective distribution systems."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严禁在协议中约定固定或最低转售价格，规避核心限制风险", "实施平台禁令须保持中立，品牌方自营与经销商政策必须一致"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Suppliers must eliminate fixed or minimum resale price clauses to avoid hardcore restriction classifications and severe penalties.", "Practice note: Ensure online marketplace restrictions are neutral; do not ban distributors from platforms where the brand itself operates.", "What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding discriminatory application of vertical restraints and proportionality assessments by national authorities."]
core_points_en: Fixed or minimum resale prices constitute hardcore restrictions and remain a primary enforcement priority for European competition authorities.
Online marketplace bans must be applied uniformly and non-discriminatorily; suppliers cannot restrict distributors while using the same platforms themselves.
This decision highlights critical compliance boundaries regarding proportionality and non-discrimination within selective distribution systems.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Suppliers must eliminate fixed or minimum resale price clauses to avoid hardcore restriction classifications and severe penalties.
Practice note: Ensure online marketplace restrictions are neutral; do not ban distributors from platforms where the brand itself operates.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding discriminatory application of vertical restraints and proportionality assessments by national authorities.

items.title: UK government confirms new subscription contracts rules for Spring 2027
items.title_zh: 英国政府确认订阅合同新规将于 2027 年春季生效
items.title_en: UK Government Confirms New Subscription Contract Rules Effective Spring 2027
items.summary: On 2 April 2026, the UK government published its long-awaited response on the new subscription contracts regime. The announcement outlines plans for sec...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府发布回应，计划依据《数字市场、竞争与消费者法案》制定二级立法，预计 2027 年春季生效。新规旨在解决每年造成 16 亿英镑损失的 unwanted 订阅问题，通过增强透明度和退出机制保护消费者。竞争与市场管理局（CMA）强调将利用现有职权执法，违规企业最高面临全球年营业额 10% 的罚款。
items.ai_summary_en: On 2 April 2026, the UK government published its response regarding the new subscription contracts regime. The announcement outlines plans for secondary legislation under the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act (DMCCA), which is expected to take effect in Spring 2027. These new rules aim to address the issue of unwanted subscriptions, currently costing consumers approximately £1.6 billion annually, by enhancing transparency and improving exit mechanisms. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has emphasized that it will continue to enforce existing powers during the transition period. Businesses found in violation of consumer protection standards could face significant penalties, including fines of up to 10% of their annual global turnover. This regulatory shift underscores the government's commitment to empowering consumers and reducing financial burdens associated with difficult-to-cancel subscriptions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英及面向英国市场的中国企业需立即审查订阅条款，确保取消流程便捷透明。建议提前对标 CMA 执法案例进行合规自查，避免因新规实施或过渡期执法行动遭受高额罚款及声誉损失。
items.ai_insight_en: Businesses operating in or targeting the UK market should immediately review their subscription terms to ensure cancellation processes are transparent and accessible. Proactive compliance assessments against current CMA enforcement actions can mitigate risks before the formal commencement of the DMCCA rules. Companies must be vigilant regarding transitional enforcement, as the regulator remains active in addressing unfair practices. Early alignment with anticipated standards helps avoid substantial financial penalties and reputational damage. Establishing robust internal controls now ensures smoother adaptation to the upcoming legal framework and demonstrates a commitment to fair consumer treatment.

items.title: VoiceAge vs HMD saga continues with constitutional complaint
items.title_zh: VoiceAge 诉 HMD 案续：HMD 向德宪法法院提起申诉
items.title_en: HMD Global Files Constitutional Complaint in German SEP Dispute with VoiceAgeEVS
items.summary: HMD Global has filed a constitutional complaint with the German Federal Constitutional Court in the ongoing SEP dispute with VoiceAgeEVS.
items.ai_summary_zh: 芬兰 HMD Global 因与 VoiceAgeEVS 的标准必要专利纠纷，向德国联邦宪法法院提起申诉。HMD 指控德国联邦最高法院拒绝将关键 FRAND 问题移交欧盟法院，侵犯了其获得法定法官审理的宪法权利。尽管欧盟委员会曾建议移交，但德国法院未予采纳，此案凸显了国内终审与欧盟法解释之间的张力。
items.ai_summary_en: HMD Global has lodged a constitutional complaint with the German Federal Constitutional Court regarding its ongoing standard-essential patent litigation with VoiceAgeEVS. The Finnish smartphone manufacturer contends that the Federal Court of Justice violated its constitutional right to a lawful judge by refusing to refer critical FRAND licensing questions to the Court of Justice of the European Union. This refusal occurred despite an amicus curiae submission from the European Commission urging such a referral. Filed on March 12, approximately six weeks after the Federal Court of Justice dismissed HMD’s appeal, the complaint highlights significant tension between national final judgments and the interpretation of EU law. Given the high threshold for admissibility before the German Constitutional Court, this development represents a strategic procedural step in the continuing dispute.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇 SEP 诉讼时，若遇德国法院拒绝移审欧盟法院，可评估宪法申诉路径。需密切关注德国宪法法院对此类程序权利的界定，以优化在欧诉讼策略并争取更有利的法律解释环境。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the procedural complexities parties face when national supreme courts decline to seek preliminary rulings from the CJEU in SEP cases. Litigants should evaluate whether constitutional complaints alleging violations of the right to a lawful judge offer viable avenues for further review, despite the high admissibility standards. The European Commission’s stance remains a relevant factor in advocating for referrals. Practitioners must closely monitor how the German Constitutional Court defines these procedural rights, as its decision could influence future strategies for managing cross-border SEP litigation and shaping the legal environment for FRAND interpretations in Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["HMD 主张德最高法院拒移审欧盟法院违宪", "案件聚焦标准必要专利许可中的 FRAND 义务解释", "欧盟委员会曾作为法庭之友支持移交审理"]

The case centers on the interpretation of FRAND licensing obligations for standard-essential patent holders under EU competition law.
